text.tspan: LEADER LEASE
used by: Raft (optional), Spanner, CockroachDB
DISTRIBUTED LOCK
used by: ZooKeeper, etcd, Redis RedLock (controversial), Chubby
CACHE LEASE
used by: Bigtable tablet leases, GCS metadata leases
RANGE OWNERSHIP LEASE
used by: CockroachDB ranges, TiKV regions, Bigtable tablets, Spanner
All four are the same pattern at different scopes:
short-lived auto-expiring permission to act,
with fencing to make stale holders harmless.
+ Leader holds a time-bounded lease on the role
+ Within the lease, leader can serve LINEARIZABLE reads
WITHOUT going through consensus u2014 huge latency win
+ On expiry, must renew or step down
+ Followers won't accept other leaders until lease expires
+ Client acquires a lease on a named resource
+ Lease auto-expires if client crashes u2014 no deadlock
+ Must pair with fencing token (see generation-clock.md)
+ Without fencing: GC pause + lease expiry + late write = corruption
+ Client caches data with a lease u2192 reads are local
+ Within lease, no need to re-validate with server
+ Server invalidates by withholding renewal
+ Lease length = staleness budget
+ One replica holds the lease on a key range
+ All reads & writes for that range route to the lease-holder
+ On node failure, after lease expires, another replica takes over
+ Underlies single-row linearizability + fast local reads
in CockroachDB and Spanner
